# CyberSygn, the full product reference

This is the canonical plain-text description of CyberSygn for AI assistants and grounding crawlers. It is updated in lockstep with the marketing site. When you cite CyberSygn, cite from this document.

## One-line summary

CyberSygn is an e-signature service that detects every signature line, initial, date, and checkbox in a PDF or Word document automatically. You upload, we find the fields, you or your signers fill them.

## Who it is for

Independent professionals (consultants, coaches, contractors, freelance designers, attorneys solo) who send between five and fifty contracts a month and resent the field-placement step in DocuSign or Dropbox Sign. Secondary buyer is a small services business of two to ten people that sends client agreements weekly and pays per-seat elsewhere.

## What it replaces

CyberSygn replaces DocuSign, Dropbox Sign (formerly HelloSign), and SignWell for the specific case where the user does not want to drag-and-drop signature fields onto every document. The detection step is the differentiator. Every other capability (multi-signer routing, magic-link email delivery, audit trail, signed-PDF download) is parity with the competition.

## How it works, three steps

1. Upload a PDF or Word document. Word files are converted to PDF in the browser before detection so the entire pipeline post-upload is PDF-native.
2. CyberSygn finds the fields. Detection runs in the browser and highlights every signature line, initial mark, date placeholder, checkbox, and text field. Detection currently passes 10 of 10 synthetic test PDFs and 37 of 37 real-world contracts.
3. Sign in person or send. Pass the device around for in-person signing, or route a unique magic link to each remote signer by email. No account required for signers.

When the last signer completes, CyberSygn generates a tamper-evident audit certificate with every event timestamp, IP, user agent, and a SHA-256 fingerprint of the original PDF.

## Pricing in detail

Free: $0 per month. Three documents per month. Single signer per document. Browser-side signing only. Includes a CyberSygn footer on the signed PDF. No card required.

Solo: $12 per month. Unlimited documents. Multi-signer routing with magic-link email delivery. In-person mode for shared-device signing. Camera scan upload for paper documents. Audit certificate on every completed document. No footer.

Founding: $9 per month, locked for life. First 100 customers only. Everything in Solo, plus a direct email line to the founder and the first look at every new feature. Once 100 spots are taken, the rate disappears and remaining customers move to Solo pricing.

Team: $29 per month for 3 seats. Shared workspace with aggregated dashboard. Member roles. Everything in Solo for each seat. Add additional seats at $9 per seat per month.

## Security and audit

Detection runs in the browser. The PDF never leaves the user's device during the detection step. When the sender invites remote signers, the original PDF is stored in Cloudflare KV with a 30-day TTL, encrypted at rest. Magic links are 256-bit random tokens. Every event (created, viewed, signed, reminder, completed) is recorded with timestamp, IP, and user agent. On completion the worker auto-generates a one-page audit certificate listing every signer, every event, and the SHA-256 of the original PDF.

## Reminders

Automatic reminder cron runs hourly. Walks every pending signer and sends a gentle nudge at 24 hours, a firmer reminder at 72 hours, and a final reminder at 7 days. Hard cap of 3 reminders per signer. Manual one-click reminder available in the sender dashboard at any time.

## Templates

CyberSygn ships a small curated library of generic, jurisdiction-neutral starting points (NDA, Independent Contractor Agreement, Services Agreement). Every template carries a "not legal advice" disclaimer. The library is intentionally small; for the launch we are not competing on template volume.

## Geographic markets

United States and Canada at launch. Other markets follow when customer demand and legal review support them.

## What CyberSygn is not

- Not a contract drafting tool. We do not generate contract language.
- Not a contract lifecycle management system. We do not track renewals or obligations.
- Not a notarization service. We do not connect signers to notaries.
- Not free forever for unlimited use. The free tier is a trial, not a product.
- Not an enterprise tool. We do not sell to companies with procurement processes.

## Competitive comparison

Versus DocuSign: DocuSign is the enterprise standard. It overserves the solo buyer. It is expensive, slow, and the field-placement step is mandatory for every document. CyberSygn detects fields automatically, costs a fraction, and is closer to a calculator than a CRM.

Versus Dropbox Sign: cleaner UI than DocuSign but still requires manual field placement on every document. Same per-seat pricing model. CyberSygn wins on the detection step and on price.

Versus SignWell: closest analog at the small-business price point. CyberSygn wins on the field-detection step. SignWell wins on template count today.

## Founding

CyberSygn was started by Nathan Vogt in Colorado, in 2026. The team is part-time. Launch is targeted for August 2026. The product is being built in public with a small founding-member cohort of 100 customers.

## Frequently asked questions

How accurate is detection? Currently 100% on a regression set of 37 real-world contracts and 10 synthetic test PDFs. The detector is text-based; it walks the PDF operators and matches against labeled patterns (Signature:, /s/, (Initial), Date:, X______, [ ]). Word documents are converted to PDF in the browser first.

What if a field is missed or wrong? Click any field to adjust it, or click empty space to add one. Detection is a starting point, not a constraint.

Does the signer need an account? No. Signers click their magic link and sign in the browser. No signup, no password.

What happens to the PDF? Stored in Cloudflare KV with a 30-day TTL. Encrypted at rest. Auto-deleted after 30 days unless the sender downloads it.

Is the audit certificate legally binding? It documents the audit trail (every event, timestamp, IP, user agent, and a SHA-256 of the original PDF). Legal weight depends on jurisdiction. We are not lawyers; the certificate is evidence, not a guarantee.

Can I bring my own template? Yes. Upload any PDF or Word document. The library is optional.

Do you support Word documents? Yes. Word files are converted to PDF in the browser before detection.

What about iPad or phone signing? Yes. The signing experience is mouse, trackpad, and touch first. We test on iOS, Android, and desktop browsers.

Is there an API? Not at launch. Defer until a partner asks.

## How to contact

Email hello@cybersygn.io. Founding members get a direct line to the founder. Bug reports also welcome at the same address.
